results.title: Top 10 AutoCAD Alternatives for Architects, Engineers, and Designers
results.content:
## Top 10 AutoCAD Alternatives

### 1. SketchUp

SketchUp is a versatile 3D modeling software known for its intuitive interface and ease of use. It offers powerful tools perfect for creating detailed models, from architectural designs and interiors to furniture and even 3D printing projects like 3D-printed houses.

Ideal for architects, interior designers, and hobbyists, SketchUp makes it easy to quickly visualize and conceptualize designs, including buildings, landscapes, and interiors. With an extensive library of pre-built models, itâs perfect for building realistic environments and showcasing ideas to clients. As a user-friendly alternative to AutoDesk, SketchUp is a go-to tool for efficient and creative 3D modeling.

Pros: [...] ### 6. TinkerCAD

TinkerCAD is not only a great tool for beginners but can also be useful for more experienced users who need to quickly create simple 3D models. Its intuitive interface and drag-and-drop tools make it easy to create basic shapes and designs. However, it may not be the best option for advanced or complex projects as it lacks some of the more robust features found in other CAD software.

One of the major advantages of TinkerCAD is its affordability â or rather, lack thereof. The software is free to use, making it an attractive choice for students or hobbyists just starting out with 3D modeling. It also runs entirely online and does not require any additional downloads or installations, making it easily accessible from any device with internet access.

Pros: [...] ### 7. NanoCAD

NanoCAD is a free and user-friendly CAD software offering various basic and advanced features for creating 2D and 3D models. It has a familiar interface similar to AutoCAD, making it easy for users to transition from one software to another. NanoCAD also offers a comprehensive set of tools for drawing, editing, and annotating designs. Additionally, it can work with multiple file formats such as DWG, DXF, and DWF.

Pros:

 User-friendly interface
 Offers both 2D and 3D modeling capabilities
 Supports multiple file formats
 Advanced features like parametric modeling and dynamic input

Cons:

 Limited advanced features compared to other CAD software
 Requires a paid subscription for technical support

MagicaVoxel is a free program, quite similar to 3D Slash, allowing to create a great artistic rendering. This program works with voxels, which are 3D pixels or âvolume pixelsâ. The selection of colors offered by this free software is a big advantage. MagicaVoxel is offering interesting design tools to make your design process as easy as possible.

### Leopoly

results.title: Choosing the Best 3D CAD Software: A Comprehensive Guide
results.content:
Price: $599 or a free simplified version

SelfCAD is a basic in-browser direct modeling environment for those wanting to get their feet wet in 3D. It offers multiple options for starting objects that the user can then modify, sculpt, or combine with other objects. SelfCAD allows some parametric control through sketching and extruding, revolving, or lofting these sketches. SVG images can be imported for custom extrusions. Details such as roundovers and chamfers can be added to individual edges in a fairly intuitive user interface with tutorial videos ready at hand. [...] ZBrush to some extent works with solids in that geometric primitives can be parametrically set up, for example, for different bracelet sizings. These are converted into sculptable meshes using the PolyMesh3D tool. With ZModeler, an essential subset of mesh editing tools, edges can be individually highlighted for making roundovers, bevels, selective creasing, or smoothening. The user can start with a wireframe skeleton and add clay to it, just like a real sculpt. Zspheres are another way of creating a base body instead of having to sculpt the proportions manually. Multiple bodies can be generated and sculpted in the same space, enabling the creation of environments rather than individual objects. Rulers and numerical input fields ensure accuracy where needed. Gem Cut Studio makes it [...] Price: $995, $195 for students

Rhinoceros is the best introduction kit for anyone intending to dive into the world of professional 3D modeling. Its tutorials are high-level right from the start as is its user interface. This is unavoidable given the unlimited modeling features and toolsets the program offers. As the userâs expertise grows, so will his or her familiarity with hotkeys and textual input methods which drastically speed up the workflow. Another nice feature is that Rhino has layers. This allows the grouping of specific selection sets, visualizing them in different colors, which altogether improves the level of organization.

DMLS / SLM Technology Aluminium Titanium Stainless Steel 316L Wax Casting Bronze Brass Silver HP Metal Binder Jetting Stainless Steel 316L [...] ### Blender

Blender is a well-known CAD software. It is a free but really powerful mesh-based direct modeling software. It is used by professionals to develop animations or even video games. Rendering, sculpting and modeling are quite easy with this 3D tool! Moreover, it is perfectly adapted to the creation of 3D printed parts. 

### Sketchup

Sketchup is also used as a professional 3D modeling software, particularly useful for architectural models. This software is a little bit complicated to use and has a large toolset. A lot of training and tutorials are available online to learn how to use this program. Free versions of this 3D software are available, but you obviously have more options by becoming a paying user of Sketchup. [...] ### What are the advantages of TinkerCAD?

TinkerCAD is a convenient software: it is free, suited for beginners, simple to use, and easy to learn. Basic editing tools, text editing, drawing, solid modeling, and rendering tools. This 3D modeling software offers parametric and free-form editing tools. TinkerCAD works by adding primitive shapes in order to create solids, all of this, through an easy user interface. To create 3D prints, TinkerCAD is actually a really good option. However, there are some other great options available on the market, and you should certainly take a look at them. 

### Start looking for alternatives!

results.content:
### Tinkercad alternatives in a nutshell

 SelfCAD â best for sculpting and manipulating shapes
 3D Slash â best for building with basic voxels
 Blender â best for professional-level design
 SketchUp â best for refining intricate shapes
 MagicaVoxel â best for building with complex voxels.

Overall, you canât go wrong with any of these Tinkercad alternatives. However, if we had to choose two, weâd choose SelfCAD and Blender.

If you enjoyed this article, read our piece on the best 3D modelling software for Macs.

### Find out more

For expert advice on CAD software and 3D modelling, please get in touch with the team at 01765 694 007, email (/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#5f2b3a3e321f3e3b3b362b36293a7227713c3032), or you can

Additive-X team memberBook an appointment with Elaine [...] While SelfCAD has a steeper learning curve than Tinkercad, the sculpting tool is simple to use, and you can use various brushes to manipulate shapes with chamfer, fillet, taper, curve, loft and revolve tools one click away.

The bottom line: SelfCAD is the best free Tinkercad alternative.

### #2 â 3D Slash

3D-Slash

Credit

3D Slash is a fresh take on model making, letting you create models from scratch by specifying the block size from which you slash (or cut) cubes.

Building models is intuitive with the subtractive approach (chiselling away blocks) as if you are building a sculpture. You can then use the âbrickworkâ tool to add more of these blocks (voxels) and create the model you want. [...] Skip to content

Letâs jump in!

### What is Tinkercad, and why is it popular?

Tinkercad is a free online computer-aided design (CAD) program suitable for creating models for 3D printing. It is popular because it is easy to use and teaches code using blocks, helping people of all ages hone their skills.

The downside to Tinkercad is its simplicity â while it is fantastic for simple models, shapes, and objects, it is found wanting when complexity ramps up.

Thankfully, there are free Tinkercad alternatives like SelfCAD and Blender (a professional-grade tool). There are also even easier tools to learn, like 3D Slash. We cover all these below.

### The best free Tinkercad alternatives
